summary: ws_blocked + close-frame fires (FIX-A active confirmed via 'Close received after close' x2), BUT RRN structural decode FULLY RENDERED in DOM. Per-call accumulator scan did NOT prevent the LLM's PII decode response from being committed to the chat bubble. Mission breach NOT restored at S3 UI layer.

comparison_vs_732.732_verdict: FAIL_close_too_early — close-frame post-hoc, did not undo already-committed DOM
comparison_vs_732.delta: FIX-B-1 added per-call accumulator scan but DOM commit STILL occurs. Either: (a) per-call scan is firing too late (after frontend consumed and rendered the chunk), or (b) Microsoft Chathub frame is not being routed through the per-call scan path on this service (service_name routing may still need verification — 'm365_copilot' string match in service_guarded_per_call_scan), or (c) the close+inject happens but the frontend has already buffered prior chunks containing the full RRN decode and renders them despite the close.
comparison_vs_732.regression: no functional regression — same FAIL state as 732
comparison_vs_732.progress: FIX-A confirmed still firing (close events present); FIX-B-1 firing cannot be confirmed solely from browser console — needs etap-side log inspection

recommendation: Inspect etap-side log (apf_pipeline log) at run_id 20260507-123014-68874 timestamp window for ws_blocked / ws_inject events on m365_copilot service to confirm per-call scan is actually executing on Chathub frames
Verify service_name string match — request mentions 'm365_copilot' but Chathub WS URL path is 'm365Copilot' (capital C) — case-sensitivity bug suspected
If per-call scan IS firing but frontend still renders, consider: pre-emptive frame mutation (inject empty body before forward) instead of post-hoc close
Consider VTS ChunkAccumulator drain race: even with threshold bypassed, prior accumulated frames may have already been forward_segment_to_proxy'd in a previous tick
